seoTitle: Good Hope vs Goodnight — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Good Hope and Goodnight. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Good Hope vs Goodnight: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Good Hope and Goodnight.
heading: Good Hope vs Goodnight — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Good Hope, New South Wales and Goodnight, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Good Hope has a lower overall climate risk score (32/100) compared to Goodnight (41/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Good Hope or Goodnight safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Good Hope has a flood risk score of 39/100, while Goodnight scores 30/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Good Hope scores 23/100 for bushfire risk. Goodnight scores 62/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Good Hope or Goodnight?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Good Hope has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.
